content.title: The problems and benefits created by the spread of the water hyacinth in Kenya

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: Water hyacinth (Eichhornia crassipes), an aquatic plant native to South America, first appeared in countries in Africa in the early 1900s. Scientists there called it the 'world's worst aquatic weed', after it spread from the southernmost tip of Africa in the early 1900s and started obstructing major dams and rivers.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: In east Africa the plant arrived with Belgian colonists in Rwanda, who liked the look of its glossy leaves and delicate purple flowers floating in their ponds. But by the 1980s, it had 'escaped' out of the country via the Kagera river and made its way downstream to Lake Victoria. There, with no natural predators and perfect temperature conditions, the plant began spreading in the open water, blocking fishing routes and providing a new habitat for disease-carrying mosquitoes.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: For the women who smoke fish from the lake to sell it has meant declining income, as the boats that once brought the fish to shore by the hundreds struggle to navigate through the mass of plants. But water hyacinth isn't their only headache. In order to smoke the fish that they buy, they must gather huge quantities of firewood, sometimes walking as far as 10km each way to collect enough to complete their work. And each day as they cook, they breathe in the thick, grey smoke. About three out of four families in Kenya depend on wood or charcoal to cook their daily meals, and the rate is even higher in rural areas, Kenya's latest demographic and health survey shows. Using solid fuels like these for cooking increases indoor pollution. The World Health Organization estimates that about 14,300 Kenyans die annually as a result of indoor air pollution - most of which is caused by cooking and heating sources.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: Some years ago, on the shores of Lake Victoria, huge piles of water hyacinth that villagers had taken out of the water in an attempt to clear it were a common sight. But buried in those decaying waxy leaves was a renewable energy gold mine. It turns out the floating plant isn't just good at spreading - its foliage also contains a high ratio of carbon to nitrogen. It's a magic combination that has captivated researchers' imaginations since as early as the 1980s when, across the world, they began to explore its potential as a biofuel. Just about 4kg of the dried plant would be enough to cater for a large family's daily energy needs, early research predicted.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: In 2014, Nigerian academics announced they had got better yields of biofuel gas when they mixed the plant with chicken manure. A few years later, Kenyan scientists confirmed what their Nigerian peers and others had already found: manure worked to improve the process of converting the weed into gas.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: In 2018, the technology came to a village on the shore of Lake Victoria, called Dunga. The project promised a two-for-one solution to the dual menaces of the water hyacinth and dependence on firewood. The community received a pair of donated biogas digesters - machines that would transform a mix of water hyacinth and cow dung into biogas for cooking.

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: The digesters work a bit like a stomach. The mixture goes in one end - think of it as a mouth - and over the next 20 to 30 days, it goes through a fermentation process and breaks down, giving off gas that comes out the other end. From there, the clean-burning gas is passed through pipes to the point of use, just like traditional domestic gas. In Dunga, the machines produce enough gas to serve about 60% of the village's population. It is used in domestic stoves and for other household tasks such as purifying water and incubating chicks.

paragraphs.label: H
paragraphs.text: The project is testing whether biogas can provide an effective alternative to firewood and charcoal in rural Kenyan communities. Results indicate that the programme seems to be working. The women who smoke the lake fish are already getting sick less often. Besides, they don't have to devote a lot of time every day to gathering firewood, which is a great relief. As a result, they're able to make more money for their families from other enterprises.

paragraphs.label: I
paragraphs.text: Kanyiva Muindi is an epidemiologist and air pollution research fellow at the African Population and Health Research Centre in Nairobi. She says families who switch to the smokeless cooking method could expect fewer respiratory diseases. Women, young girls and children are particularly vulnerable because they are the ones who cook in the kitchen or outside over fires.

paragraphs.label: J
paragraphs.text: How much better the biogas stoves will be for the community's health still needs more research, says Dominic Kahumbu Wanjihia, Biogas International's chief executive. But unless the price of the machines drops, it's pretty clear that most communities will never be able to afford any, since they sell for about $750.

paragraphs.label: K
paragraphs.text: Kanyiva says affordability is a challenge worth addressing, given the huge health and environmental dangers posed by 'dirty' fuels such as wood, charcoal and kerosene. If biogas could become affordable on a large scale, she says it 'would be life-changing for millions on the African continent and beyond'.

groups.desc:
Questions 1-7
Do the following statements agree with the information given in Reading Passage 1?
In boxes 1-7 on your answer sheet, write
TRUE if the statement agrees with the information
FALSE if the statement contradicts the information
NOT GIVEN if there is no information on this

questions.title: Water hyacinth was introduced as a decorative plant in east Africa.
questions.analysis:
主体匹配：the plant = water hyacinth，完全对应；
地点匹配：In east Africa = 题干 East Africa；
带入来源：arrived with Belgian colonists 对应 was brought to；
目的逻辑推导：
liked the look of its glossy leaves and delicate purple flowers floating in their ponds
人们因为外形、花叶好看放在池塘观赏，观赏、美化池塘 = decorative purposes（装饰用途），语义完全等价，无相反、无缺失信息。

central_sentences.analysis: 原文表明比利时殖民者将水葫芦带入东非卢旺达，因欣赏其花叶外观、放置池塘观赏，对应题干 “引入用作装饰”，全部信息与原文一致，符合 TRUE 判定规则，答案 TRUE。
central_sentences.keywords: water hyacinth, East Africa, brought, decorative purposes
sentences: In east Africa the plant arrived with Belgian colonists in Rwanda, who liked the look of its glossy leaves and delicate purple flowers floating in their ponds.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: liked the look of its glossy leaves and delicate purple flowers floating in their ponds = decorative purposes； the plant = water hyacinth ；arrived with colonists = was brought to

central_sentences.sentence: In east Africa the plant arrived with Belgian colonists in Rwanda, who liked the look of its glossy leaves and delicate purple flowers floating in their ponds.

questions.title: Fishermen took some water hyacinth plants to Lake Victoria.
questions.analysis: 题干称“Fishermen took some water hyacinth plants to Lake Victoria”，但原文第二段明确指出水葫芦是“escaped out of the country via the Kagera river and made its way downstream to Lake Victoria”，即通过河流自然扩散，并非由渔民带入，因此答案为FALSE。

central_sentences.analysis: 水葫芦是经河流自然扩散，非渔民带入。
sentences: But by the 1980s, it had 'escaped' out of the country via the Kagera river and made its way downstream to Lake Victoria.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: Fishermen took -> escaped via Kagera river

central_sentences.sentence: But by the 1980s, it had 'escaped' out of the country via the Kagera river and made its way downstream to Lake Victoria.

questions.title: It is now difficult to force boats through the thick water hyacinth on Lake Victoria.
questions.analysis: 题干中“It is now difficult to force boats through the thick water hyacinth”对应原文第三段“boats... struggle to navigate through the mass of plants”，说明船只确实难以穿越密集的水葫芦，因此答案为TRUE。

central_sentences.analysis: 船只难以穿越密集的水葫芦。
sentences: For the women who smoke fish from the lake to sell it has meant declining income, as the boats that once brought the fish to shore by the hundreds struggle to navigate through the mass of plants.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: difficult to force boats through -> struggle to navigate through the mass of plants

central_sentences.sentence: For the women who smoke fish from the lake to sell it has meant declining income, as the boats that once brought the fish to shore by the hundreds struggle to navigate through the mass of plants.

questions.title: Chemicals produced by the water hyacinth plants are affecting the numbers of fish in Lake Victoria.
questions.analysis: 题干提到“Chemicals produced by the water hyacinth... affecting fish numbers”，但全文未提及水葫芦释放化学物质或对鱼类数量造成影响，仅提到其阻碍航道和滋生蚊虫，因此答案为NOT GIVEN。

central_sentences.reason: 无相关信息。
central_sentences.analysis: 原文未提及化学物质影响鱼数量。

questions.title: Cooking with charcoal has been proved to be even worse for people's health than cooking with wood.
questions.analysis: 题干比较木炭与木材对健康的危害程度，但原文第三段仅指出使用固体燃料（如木材或木炭）会增加室内污染，并引用WHO数据说明死亡人数，未比较两者危害大小，因此答案为NOT GIVEN。

central_sentences.reason: 无相关信息。
central_sentences.analysis: 原文未比较木炭与木材的健康影响。

questions.title: People found it impossible to remove much water hyacinth from Lake Victoria.
questions.analysis: 题干称“People found it impossible to remove much water hyacinth”，但原文第四段提到“huge piles of water hyacinth that villagers had taken out of the water... were a common sight”，说明村民曾大量清除水葫芦，因此题干说法错误，答案为FALSE。

central_sentences.analysis: 村民曾大量清除水葫芦，堆成大堆。
sentences: Some years ago, on the shores of Lake Victoria, huge piles of water hyacinth that villagers had taken out of the water in an attempt to clear it were a common sight.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: impossible to remove much -> huge piles ... were a common sight

central_sentences.sentence: Some years ago, on the shores of Lake Victoria, huge piles of water hyacinth that villagers had taken out of the water in an attempt to clear it were a common sight.

questions.title: Scientists started investigating the possibility of using water hyacinth to generate biogas in the last century.
questions.analysis: 题干中“Scientists started investigating... in the last century”对应原文第四段“since as early as the 1980s when... they began to explore its potential as a biofuel”，1980年代属于上世纪，因此答案为TRUE。

central_sentences.analysis: 1980年代科学家已开始研究其产沼气潜力。
sentences: It's a magic combination that has captivated researchers' imaginations since as early as the 1980s when, across the world, they began to explore its potential as a biofuel.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: last century -> as early as the 1980s

central_sentences.sentence: It's a magic combination that has captivated researchers' imaginations since as early as the 1980s when, across the world, they began to explore its potential as a biofuel.

groups.title: Generating biogas for domestic use in Dunga
groups.desc:
Questions 8-10
Complete the flow-chart below.
Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer.
Write your answers in boxes 8-10 on your answer sheet.
groups.collect:
Generating biogas for domestic use in Dunga
First, place water hyacinth together with some $40320$ into a digester.
Leave the mixture until the $40321$ is completed.
Capture the gas emitted by the digester and use $40322$ to transport it to individual homes.
Then use the gas for cooking as well as making water fit for human consumption.

questions.analysis: 题干要求填写与水葫芦一起放入消化器的物质。原文第六段明确指出“a mix of water hyacinth and cow dung into biogas for cooking”，因此答案为“cow dung”。

central_sentences.analysis: 混合物为水葫芦和牛粪。
sentences: The community received a pair of donated biogas digesters - machines that would transform a mix of water hyacinth and cow dung into biogas for cooking.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: cow dung = cow dung

central_sentences.sentence: The community received a pair of donated biogas digesters - machines that would transform a mix of water hyacinth and cow dung into biogas for cooking.

questions.analysis: 题干要求填写混合物在消化器中完成的过程。原文第七段提到“it goes through a fermentation process and breaks down”，因此答案为“fermentation”或“fermentation process”，符合不超过两个词的要求。

central_sentences.analysis: 混合物需经发酵过程分解。
sentences: The mixture goes in one end - think of it as a mouth - and over the next 20 to 30 days, it goes through a fermentation process and breaks down, giving off gas that comes out the other end.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: fermentation process = fermentation process

central_sentences.sentence: The mixture goes in one end - think of it as a mouth - and over the next 20 to 30 days, it goes through a fermentation process and breaks down, giving off gas that comes out the other end.

questions.analysis: 题干询问气体如何被输送到家庭。原文第七段指出“the clean-burning gas is passed through pipes to the point of use”，因此答案为“pipes”，完全匹配原文用词。

central_sentences.analysis: 气体通过管道输送至用户。
sentences: From there, the clean-burning gas is passed through pipes to the point of use, just like traditional domestic gas.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: pipes = pipes

central_sentences.sentence: From there, the clean-burning gas is passed through pipes to the point of use, just like traditional domestic gas.

groups.title: Cooking with biogas in Dunga
groups.desc:
Questions 11-13
Complete the notes below.
Choose ONE WORD ONLY from the passage for each answer.
Write your answers in boxes 11-13 on your answer sheet.
groups.collect:
Cooking with biogas in Dunga

Benefits for the women in the village of cooking with biogas

  • no need for them to spend so much $40323$ collecting fuel
  • they can focus on different tasks that bring in $40324$
  • they are less likely to experience certain diseases connected to burning wood

Drawbacks of changing to biogas

  • the $40325$ of the digesters is beyond the reach of most villages

questions.analysis: 题干要求填写女性不再需要大量花费的东西。原文第八段提到“they don't have to devote a lot of time every day to gathering firewood”，说明节省的是“time”，因此答案为“time”。

central_sentences.analysis: 使用沼气节省了收集燃料的时间。
sentences: Besides, they don't have to devote a lot of time every day to gathering firewood, which is a great relief.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: spend so much time -> don't have to devote a lot of time every day to gathering firewood

central_sentences.sentence: Besides, they don't have to devote a lot of time every day to gathering firewood, which is a great relief.

questions.analysis: 题干要求填写女性可通过其他任务获得的东西。原文第八段末句“As a result, they're able to make more money for their families from other enterprises”明确指出是“money”，因此答案为“money”。

central_sentences.analysis: 节省时间后可从事其他赚钱活动。
sentences: As a result, they're able to make more money for their families from other enterprises.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: make more money = money

central_sentences.sentence: As a result, they're able to make more money for their families from other enterprises.

questions.analysis: 题干要求填写阻碍村庄采用沼气的障碍。原文第九段提到“unless the price of the machines drops... most communities will never be able to afford any”，说明问题是“price”，因此答案为“price”。

central_sentences.analysis: 消化器价格高昂，多数村庄负担不起。
sentences: But unless the price of the machines drops, it's pretty clear that most communities will never be able to afford any, since they sell for about $750.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: price = price

central_sentences.sentence: But unless the price of the machines drops, it's pretty clear that most communities will never be able to afford any, since they sell for about $750.

content.title: How could multilingualism benefit India's poorest schoolchildren?
content.notes: ['* slum: a very densely populated area in which the infrastructure is incomplete and services inadequate or non-existent']

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: The crowded and bustling streets of Delhi teem with life. Stop to listen and, above the din of rickshaws and buses, you'll hear a multitude of languages, as more than 20 million people go about their daily lives. Many were born and raised here, and many millions more have recently made India's capital their home, having moved from surrounding neighbourhoods, cities and states or across the country, often in the hope of gaining better jobs and a better life. Some arrive speaking fluent Hindi, the dominant language in Delhi (and the official language of government), but many arrive speaking any number of India's 22 officially recognised languages, let alone the hundreds of regional languages in a country of more than 1.3 billion people.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: A team of researchers led by Professor Ianthi Tsimpli of Cambridge University is currently working on a project collecting data on 1,000 primary-age children in Delhi and the cities of Hyderabad and Bihar. The overriding aim of the four-year project, called 'Multilingualism and Multiliteracy', is to find out why in a country where multilingualism is so common (more than 255 million people in India speak at least two languages, and nearly 90 million speak three or more languages), the many benefits of speaking more than one language, observed in schools in Europe for instance, do not apply to many of India's schoolchildren.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: 'Each year across India, 600,000 children are tested, and year after year over 50% of children in Standard 5 [ten-year-olds] cannot read a Standard 2 [seven-year-olds] task fluently, and just under 50% of them cannot solve a Standard 2 subtraction task,' says Tsimpli. She explains that low educational achievement can lead to many of these students dropping out of school - a problem disproportionately affecting female students.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: Tsimpli and her colleagues are investigating whether these low learning outcomes could be caused by an Indian school system where the language that children are taught in often differs from the language used at home. The research project, which focuses on 8 to 11-year-old schoolchildren in rural and urban areas, collects data on whether the schoolchildren live in slum or non-slum areas. Many of the children have moved from remote, rural areas to urban areas. They are so poor they have to live in slums and, as a result of migration, they may speak languages that are different from the regional language.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: Having already tested 1,000 children, the researchers will now embark on retesting them. They intend to look not only at test results, but also at variables such as the standard of schooling, the environment and the teaching practices themselves. It's possible that one of the causes of low performance is the lack of pupil-centred teaching methods; in many Indian primary schools the teacher dominates and there is little room for independent learning.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: Although the findings are at a preliminary stage, Tsimpli and her team have found that the medium of instruction used in schools, especially English, may hold back those children who have little familiarity with, or exposure to, the language before starting school and outside of school life. According to Tsimpli, most of the evidence from this and other projects shows that English instruction for children from low socio-economic areas might not be the best way for them to learn, at least in the first three years of primary education.

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: 'What we would recommend for everyone, not just low socio-economic status children, would be to start learning in the language they feel comfortable learning in ... English can still be used, but perhaps not as the medium of instruction in primary schools. It could, for example, be one of the subjects that are being taught alongside other subjects. We are not suggesting that English be withdrawn - that ship has sailed - but we perhaps have to think more about learner needs. There is perhaps too much uniformity in teaching and less tailoring to the children's language abilities and needs,' says Tsimpli.

paragraphs.label: H
paragraphs.text: While the preliminary results show there is no difference in general intelligence among boys and girls from slum areas versus those from urban poor backgrounds, an unanticipated finding has been that children from slum backgrounds do not seem to lag behind children from other urban poor backgrounds - and in some cases outperform them (e.g. in numeracy and literacy tasks). According to the researchers, this unexpected finding may be down to the life experiences of children growing up in slums. They are likely to mature faster and come into closer contact with the numeracy skills essential for day-to-day survival.

paragraphs.label: I
paragraphs.text: The project has already caught the attention of government ministers, who are keen to use the findings of the study to inform and adjust school policy in Delhi and the wider state. 'They are as keen as us to understand how the challenging context of deprivation can be attenuated when focusing on the languages children learn and use while at school. Our findings don't mean you're doomed if you're poor. It may be that these low learning outcomes are because of the way education is provided in India, with a huge focus on Hindi and English as the mediums of instruction, to the potential detriment of children unfamiliar with those languages,' explains Tsimpli.

paragraphs.label: J
paragraphs.text: 'Language is central to the way knowledge is transferred - so the medium of instruction is obviously hugely influential. We hope to ... show that problem solving, numeracy and literacy can and do improve in children who are educated in a language of instruction they know. The trick may be to bridge school skills with life skills and make use of the richness of a child's life experience to help them learn in the most effective ways possible,' says Tsimpli.

groups.title: Multilingualism in Delhi
groups.desc:
Questions 14-19
Complete the summary using the list of words, A-J, below.
Write the correct letter, A-J, in boxes 14-19 on your answer sheet.
groups.collect:
Multilingualism in Delhi

The city of Delhi has a $40326$ and as you walk through its streets you hear people speaking a variety of languages. Some of them have spent their entire life in Delhi, while others are $40327$. Whether they have come from a $40328$ or have travelled from the other side of India, they have all come in search of things such as improved $40329$.

A team of researchers led by Professor Ianthi Tsimpli of Cambridge University is collecting data on primary-age schoolchildren in Delhi and other Indian cities. The $40330$ of the research is to discover why multilingual Indian schoolchildren do not experience $40331$ to those that multilingual schoolchildren in Europe experience.

questions.analysis: 题干中的“a _____”描述德里城市的基本特征，原文首段提到“The crowded and bustling streets of Delhi teem with life”以及“more than 20 million people go about their daily lives”，说明德里人口稠密。选项 I “dense population”正是对这一描述的准确概括，因此答案为 I。

central_sentences.reason: 原文明确描述德里街道拥挤、人口超2000万，与'dense population'匹配
central_sentences.analysis: 首句描述德里人口稠密，对应选项I
sentences: The crowded and bustling streets of Delhi teem with life.
Stop to listen and, above the din of rickshaws and buses, you'll hear a multitude of languages, as more than 20 million people go about their daily lives.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: dense population -> crowded and bustling streets of Delhi teem with life; more than 20 million people

central_sentences.sentence: The crowded and bustling streets of Delhi teem with life.
Stop to listen and, above the din of rickshaws and buses, you'll hear a multitude of languages, as more than 20 million people go about their daily lives.

questions.analysis: 题干中“others are _____”指代那些并非在德里出生的人群。原文明确指出“many millions more have recently made India's capital their home”，说明他们是近期迁入的新移民。选项 J “new immigrants”与原文“recently made...their home”形成同义替换，因此答案为 J。

central_sentences.reason: 原文明确指出大量人recently迁入德里，符合J 'new immigrants'
central_sentences.analysis: 'new immigrants'对应recently moved to Delhi的人群
central_sentences.keywords: many millions more, made India's capital their home
sentences: Many were born and raised here, and many millions more have recently made India's capital their home, having moved from surrounding neighbourhoods, cities and states or across the country, often in the hope of gaining better jobs and a better life.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: others are -> many millions more have recently made India's capital their home

central_sentences.sentence: Many were born and raised here, and many millions more have recently made India's capital their home, having moved from surrounding neighbourhoods, cities and states or across the country, often in the hope of gaining better jobs and a better life.

questions.analysis: 题干要求填入人们迁往德里的来源地之一。原文提到新移民“having moved from surrounding neighbourhoods, cities and states or across the country”，其中“surrounding neighbourhoods”对应选项 H “nearby district”，是对邻近区域的合理概括，因此答案为 H。

central_sentences.reason: 'surrounding neighbourhoods'等短语支持H 'nearby district'作为来源地之一
central_sentences.analysis: 'nearby district'概括了surrounding neighbourhoods等...
sentences: Many were born and raised here, and many millions more have recently made India's capital their home, having moved from surrounding neighbourhoods, cities and states or across the country, often in the hope of gaining better jobs and a better life.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: from a nearby district -> from surrounding neighbourhoods, cities and states

central_sentences.sentence: Many were born and raised here, and many millions more have recently made India's capital their home, having moved from surrounding neighbourhoods, cities and states or across the country, often in the hope of gaining better jobs and a better life.

questions.analysis: 题干中的“improved _____”指人们迁往德里的目的。原文明确说明他们迁移是“in the hope of gaining better jobs and a better life”，其中“better jobs”直接对应选项 B “employment opportunities”，因此答案为 B。

central_sentences.reason: 原文明确提到迁移动机是'better jobs'，即B选项
central_sentences.analysis: 'better jobs'直接对应B 'employment opportunities'
central_sentences.keywords: better jobs and a better life
sentences: Many were born and raised here, and many millions more have recently made India's capital their home, having moved from surrounding neighbourhoods, cities and states or across the country, often in the hope of gaining better jobs and a better life.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: improved employment opportunities -> better jobs

central_sentences.sentence: Many were born and raised here, and many millions more have recently made India's capital their home, having moved from surrounding neighbourhoods, cities and states or across the country, often in the hope of gaining better jobs and a better life.

questions.analysis: 题干问研究的“_____ of the research”，原文第二段提到该项目的“overriding aim”，意为“首要目标”。选项 E “primary objective”与“overriding aim”构成标准同义替换，准确反映研究的核心目的，因此答案为 E。

central_sentences.reason: overriding aim 与 primary objective 同义。
central_sentences.analysis: 原文'overriding aim'即首要目标。
sentences: The overriding aim of the four-year project, called 'Multilingualism and Multiliteracy', is to find out why in a country where multilingualism is so common (more than 255 million people in India speak at least two languages, and nearly 90 million speak three or more languages), the many benefits of speaking more than one language, observed in schools in Europe for instance, do not apply to many of India's schoolchildren.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: primary objective -> overriding aim

central_sentences.sentence: The overriding aim of the four-year project, called 'Multilingualism and Multiliteracy', is to find out why in a country where multilingualism is so common (more than 255 million people in India speak at least two languages, and nearly 90 million speak three or more languages), the many benefits of speaking more than one language, observed in schools in Europe for instance, do not apply to many of India's schoolchildren.

questions.analysis: 题干指出印度多语儿童未获得与欧洲多语儿童“_____ to those...in Europe”。原文第二段提到“the many benefits...observed in schools in Europe...do not apply to many of India's schoolchildren”，说明两者所获益处不同。选项 F “similar advantages”准确概括了这种对比关系，因此答案为 F。

central_sentences.reason: 印度儿童未经历类似好处，即没有similar advantages。
central_sentences.analysis: 原文指出欧洲的多语言优势不适用于印度儿童。
sentences: The many benefits of speaking more than one language, observed in schools in Europe for instance, do not apply to many of India's schoolchildren.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: similar advantages -> the many benefits...do not apply

central_sentences.sentence: The many benefits of speaking more than one language, observed in schools in Europe for instance, do not apply to many of India's schoolchildren.

questions.title: Ten-year-old Indian schoolchildren tend to perform better in literacy tests than in numeracy tests.
questions.analysis:
读写不及格：>50%（一半以上人读不好）
算术不及格：<50%（不到一半人算不好）
说明更多孩子算术能过关、读写过不了关，算术水平整体强于读写，和题干说法反过来了，所以不能选 YES，只能选 NO。

central_sentences.reason: 阅读任务失败率更高，说明阅读表现更差。
central_sentences.analysis: 读写达标率 < 算术达标率 → 孩子算术表现优于读写表现。与题干表述相反，故NO
sentences: 'Each year across India, 600,000 children are tested, and year after year over 50% of children in Standard 5 [ten-year-olds] cannot read a Standard 2 [seven-year-olds] task fluently, and just under 50% of them cannot solve a Standard 2 subtraction task,' says Tsimpli.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: read=literacy；subtraction=numberacy

central_sentences.sentence: 'Each year across India, 600,000 children are tested, and year after year over 50% of children in Standard 5 [ten-year-olds] cannot read a Standard 2 [seven-year-olds] task fluently, and just under 50% of them cannot solve a Standard 2 subtraction task,' says Tsimpli.

questions.title: Tsimpli had problems convincing some female students to take part in the study.
questions.analysis: 题干提到“Tsimpli 难以说服一些女生参与研究”，但全文未提及任何关于招募困难或性别参与障碍的内容。尽管第三段提到女生辍学问题更严重，但并未涉及研究参与情况。因此信息未给出，答案为 NOT GIVEN。

central_sentences.reason: 原文没有相关信息
central_sentences.analysis: 文章未提及说服女学生参与研究的困难

questions.title: Tsimpli and her team wanted to know if there is a connection between poor academic performance and being taught in an unfamiliar language.
questions.analysis: 题干询问研究者是否想探究“学业表现差与使用不熟悉语言教学之间是否存在关联”。原文第四段明确指出研究正在调查“low learning outcomes could be caused by...language that children are taught in often differs from the language used at home”，这与题干完全一致，因此答案为 YES。

central_sentences.reason: 直接对应题干内容。
central_sentences.analysis: 原文明确调查低学习成果是否由教学语言与家庭语言不同导致。
central_sentences.keywords: investigating, low learning outcomes, language differs
sentences: Tsimpli and her colleagues are investigating whether these low learning outcomes could be caused by an Indian school system where the language that children are taught in often differs from the language used at home.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: connection between poor performance and being taught in unfamiliar language -> investigating whether low learning outcomes caused by language mismatch

central_sentences.sentence: Tsimpli and her colleagues are investigating whether these low learning outcomes could be caused by an Indian school system where the language that children are taught in often differs from the language used at home.

questions.title: The researchers have decided against investigating the impact teaching methodology may have on learning outcomes.
questions.analysis: 题干称“研究者决定不调查教学方法对学习成果的影响”，但原文第五段明确表示他们将考察“teaching practices themselves”，并推测“lack of pupil-centred teaching methods”可能是低表现的原因之一。这说明他们确实计划调查教学方法，因此题干与原文矛盾，答案为 NO。

central_sentences.reason: 明确提到会考察教学实践。
central_sentences.analysis: 原文表示将考察教学实践，并非决定不调查。
sentences: They intend to look not only at test results, but also at variables such as the standard of schooling, the environment and the teaching practices themselves.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: decided against investigating impact of teaching methodology -> intend to look at teaching practices

central_sentences.sentence: They intend to look not only at test results, but also at variables such as the standard of schooling, the environment and the teaching practices themselves.

groups.desc:
Questions 24-26
Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.

questions.title: What point does the writer make about primary schools in India in the sixth paragraph?

options.text: Exposure to English outside of school is of limited benefit.
Children learn English more easily when they are well motivated.
Poor children may be disadvantaged further by being instructed in English.
There is little consistency across schools with regard to instruction in English.

questions.analysis: 第六段指出，在低社会经济背景地区使用英语教学“might not be the best way for them to learn”，尤其在小学前几年。这表明以英语为教学媒介可能进一步损害贫困儿童的学习机会。选项 C “Poor children may be disadvantaged further by being instructed in English”准确反映了这一观点，因此答案为 C。

central_sentences.reason: 第六段支持选项C。
central_sentences.analysis: 原文指出英语教学可能阻碍缺乏接触的孩子，尤其是低收入背景儿童。
sentences: Although the findings are at a preliminary stage, Tsimpli and her team have found that the medium of instruction used in schools, especially English, may hold back those children who have little familiarity with, or exposure to, the language before starting school and outside of school life.

According to Tsimpli, most of the evidence from this and other projects shows that English instruction for children from low socio-economic areas might not be the best way for them to learn, at least in the first three years of primary education.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: poor children disadvantaged by English instruction -> English instruction may hold back children with little exposure

central_sentences.sentence: Although the findings are at a preliminary stage, Tsimpli and her team have found that the medium of instruction used in schools, especially English, may hold back those children who have little familiarity with, or exposure to, the language before starting school and outside of school life.

According to Tsimpli, most of the evidence from this and other projects shows that English instruction for children from low socio-economic areas might not be the best way for them to learn, at least in the first three years of primary education.

questions.title: What is Tsimpli suggesting when she uses the phrase 'that ship has sailed'?

options.text: The findings of the report may be of little help to some Indian schoolchildren.
Instruction in English could be better adapted to the needs of schoolchildren.
Schools have had limited success in teaching English as a separate subject.
It is too late to remove English completely as a language of instruction in schools.

questions.analysis: Tsimpli 使用“that ship has sailed”意指取消英语教学已不现实。结合上下文“We are not suggesting that English be withdrawn”，说明她承认英语作为教学语言的地位难以逆转。选项 D “It is too late to remove English completely as a language of instruction in schools”准确解释了该习语的含义，因此答案为 D。

central_sentences.reason: 直接对应
central_sentences.analysis: 短语意指英语作为教学语言已广泛普及，无法撤回，对应D。
sentences: We are not suggesting that English be withdrawn - that ship has sailed - but we perhaps have to think more about learner needs.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: too late to remove English -> not suggesting English be withdrawn - that ship has sailed

central_sentences.sentence: We are not suggesting that English be withdrawn - that ship has sailed - but we perhaps have to think more about learner needs.

questions.title: In the eighth paragraph, what do we learn has surprised researchers?

options.text: Boys and girls from low socio-economic groups have similar general intelligence levels.
The age at which children move into a slum does not affect their academic performance.
Slum children and children from other urban poor backgrounds have similar life experiences.
The literacy and numeracy skills of slum children are not lower than those of children from other urban poor backgrounds.

questions.analysis: 第八段提到一个“unanticipated finding”：贫民窟儿童在读写和算术任务上“do not seem to lag behind...and in some cases outperform them”。这与普遍预期相反，令研究者感到意外。选项 D 准确概括了这一发现，即贫民窟儿童的能力不低于其他城市贫困儿童，因此答案为 D。

central_sentences.reason: 选项D符合这一意外发现。
central_sentences.analysis: 原文意外发现贫民窟儿童在读写算方面不落后于其他城市贫困儿童。
central_sentences.keywords: unanticipated finding, slum, not lag behind
sentences: While the preliminary results show there is no difference in general intelligence among boys and girls from slum areas versus those from urban poor backgrounds, an unanticipated finding has been that children from slum backgrounds do not seem to lag behind children from other urban poor backgrounds - and in some cases outperform them (e.g. in numeracy and literacy tasks).
central_sentences.paraphrasing: slum children's skills not lower than other urban poor -> children from slum backgrounds do not seem to lag behind

central_sentences.sentence: While the preliminary results show there is no difference in general intelligence among boys and girls from slum areas versus those from urban poor backgrounds, an unanticipated finding has been that children from slum backgrounds do not seem to lag behind children from other urban poor backgrounds - and in some cases outperform them (e.g. in numeracy and literacy tasks).

content.title: The Globemakers: The Curious Story of an Ancient Craft
content.notes: ['* cartographer: someone involved in the science or pactice of drawing maps', '** plaster of Paris: a quick-setting plaster consisting of a fine white powder that hardens when moistened and allowed to dry']

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: In 2008, Peter Bellerby, who lived in London, wanted to give his father a model globe for his eightieth birthday. What seemed simple enough to start with triggered an almost obsessive, decade-long journey, marked by a series of obstacles that would have deterred anyone less determined. It ended with his establishing the world's only bespoke globemaking company.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: The first surprise in The Globemakers, Bellerby's account of this impulsive enterprise, is that obtaining such a globe was not simply a matter of a quick online order and a repressed sigh at the shipping costs. After all, contrary to stubbornly held popular views of our ancestors' geographical ignorance, we have known that the world is spherical since at least the 6th century BCE. The ancient Greek philosopher Plato in his work Phaedo likened it to a leather ball, while the accolade of producing the first recorded globe goes to the ancient Greek philosopher Crates of Mallus, who is said to have made one in around 150 BCE. Surely, Bellerby reasoned, a good-quality globe wouldn't be difficult to find.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: Nearly two millennia later, however, it seemed that the art of globemaking had been largely forgotten. Bellerby came across shoddy commercial versions designed for school classrooms and genuine antiques in auction houses that would have bust his budget. Even his trips to Morocco and India, where surely the knowledge of artisan cartographers had been preserved, drew a blank.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: Not one to be easily thwarted, Bellerby decided to make his own good-quality globe. In the process, almost everything that could possibly go wrong did so. Even the shape of the Earth posed a problem, as it is not quite a perfect sphere, but oblate (slightly flattened at the poles). Having decided to compromise and opt for two half-spherical pieces that could be fitted together, he was unable to discover anyone capable of casting moulds with sufficient accuracy to ensure that he would not be left with two half-spheres that were not quite the same circumference. Even after he eventually resolved this issue, extracting these from the moulds resulted in piles of cracked plaster of Paris and clouds of choking dust in the workshop he had set up at the rear of his house.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: This series of abortive experiments taught Bellerby a lot about the challenges of making globes, which he communicates here to the reader. Finding just the right way to prise the globes from the mould - a high-end air compressor finally did the trick - and locating the right paper and inks with which to make the gores (the sections of flat sheet mapping that are pasted onto the spherical globe) without the ink seeping out to create a mushy, unreadable mess took months and an alarming chunk out of his bank balance. Bellerby's frustration at the painstaking process of attaching the gores to the globe surface - after having found a glue with precisely the right adhesive qualities - is palpable. Right at the end of the process, he learnt that the paper had stretched slightly and so the final one overlapped the first by a centimetre (which may not seem a great deal, but when that represents 2 per cent of the Earth's diameter, it's equivalent to obliterating the Himalayas or wiping out Chile).

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: Bellerby's account of the technical challenges of globe production is interspersed with a series of interludes on great globemakers of the past and cartographic history in general. Purists might wish for more map-making details, but Bellerby clearly found a kindred spirit in Martin Behaim. He was the Nuremberg entrepreneur who in 1492 created the Erdapfel, the world's oldest surviving globe, beautifully finished by a workshop of painters and other craftsmen, only to find that the explorer Christopher Columbus had stumbled upon the Americas the very same year, rendering his masterpiece instantly out of date. Something of Bellerby's unflinching ambition is reflected in the even more heroic efforts of the Italian cartographer Vincenzo Coronelli, who, in the seventeenth century, created two globes for Louis XIV of France. It took him twenty years to complete the monstrous pair, whose vast bulk - each with a diameter of around four metres - can still be admired in the National Library of France in Paris.

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: Although a celebration of the revival of an ancient craft, Bellerby's book is also a lament for the fading away of centuries-old traditions. When he embarked on his globemaking odyssey, he struggled to find artisans with the skills to make the right moulds for the globes or foundries that could shape the meridians (the metal frames which girdle globes) in just the right way. Although he finally located the right craftsmen, some simply dropping in, serendipitously, to his workshop (by now in more suitable premises than his back room), many of these have now retired or passed away.

paragraphs.label: H
paragraphs.text: Bellerby's father finally did receive his eightieth birthday present, albeit two years late. Bellerby went on to found a company which now turns out over six hundred globes a year for customers who can have their own tiny village marked or more unusual requests fulfilled. His book, beautifully illustrated with photographs of the various stages of his venture and a few illustrations of historic globes and maps, is hardly a blueprint for commercial success. But it is more than enough to stir up admiration for the craftsmanship of the great mapmakers of the past and the obsessive determination of a modern successor who revived their almost moribund art.

groups.title: A birthday gift
groups.desc:
Questions 27-32
Complete the summary using the list of words, A-J, below.
Write the correct letter, A-J, in boxes 27-32 on your answer sheet.
groups.collect:
A birthday gift

Peter Bellerby's plan to give his father a globe for his birthday was an unexpectedly $40339$ for which he had to overcome $40340$.

He soon learnt that a straightforward $40341$ would not be possible. Some $40342$ that had been intended for $40343$ were available, as were some expensive antique globes, but these were beyond his budget. He even travelled to places where people might still have the $40344$, but Bellerby could not find what he wanted.

questions.analysis: 题干中的“unexpectedly”对应原文中的“What seemed simple enough to start with triggered an almost obsessive, decade-long journey”，说明原本简单的送礼计划演变成了一项长期而艰巨的任务。选项G“challenging task”准确概括了这一转变，因此是正确答案。

What seemed simple enough to start with triggered an almost obsessive, decade-long journey, marked by a series of obstacles that would have deterred anyone less determined.

What seemed simple enough to start with triggered an almost obsessive, decade-long journey, marked by a series of obstacles that would have deterred anyone less determined.

questions.analysis: 题干要求填入Bellerby需要克服的内容，原文提到他遭遇了“a series of obstacles”以及“almost everything that could possibly go wrong did so”，表明他面对的是大量困难。选项J“numerous problems”与这些描述完全对应，因此为正确答案。

sentences: What seemed simple enough to start with triggered an almost obsessive, decade-long journey, marked by a series of obstacles that would have deterred anyone less determined.
In the process, almost everything that could possibly go wrong did so.

central_sentences.sentence: What seemed simple enough to start with triggered an almost obsessive, decade-long journey, marked by a series of obstacles that would have deterred anyone less determined.
In the process, almost everything that could possibly go wrong did so.

questions.title: The assumption today that people in the past knew very little about geography is correct.
questions.analysis: 题干陈述“人们过去地理知识匮乏的假设是正确的”，但原文明确用“contrary to stubbornly held popular views of our ancestors' geographical ignorance”反驳了这一观点，并指出人类早在公元前6世纪就已知地球是球体。因此该陈述与作者观点矛盾，答案为NO。

central_sentences.reason: contrary to 表明作者反对该假设，故答案为 NO

sentences: After all, contrary to stubbornly held popular views of our ancestors' geographical ignorance, we have known that the world is spherical since at least the 6th century BCE.

central_sentences.sentence: After all, contrary to stubbornly held popular views of our ancestors' geographical ignorance, we have known that the world is spherical since at least the 6th century BCE.

questions.title: Plato was criticised for saying the world was shaped like a leather ball.
questions.analysis: 题干称“Plato因将地球比作皮革球而受到批评”，但原文仅客观陈述“Plato in his work Phaedo likened it to a leather ball”，并未提及任何批评或负面评价。文中无相关信息支持或否定此说法，因此答案为NOT GIVEN。

questions.title: The globe made by Crates of Mallus was an accurate representation of the known world.
questions.analysis: 题干询问Crates of Mallus制作的地球仪是否准确反映了当时已知世界，但原文只提到他是“producing the first recorded globe”，并未对其准确性作出任何评价。因此无法判断作者是否认为其准确，答案为NOT GIVEN。

central_sentences.reason: 无准确性描述。

questions.title: Bellerby assumed he would have few problems locating a well-made globe.
questions.analysis: 题干说“Bellerby原以为找一个高质量地球仪不会太难”，原文第二段明确写道“Surely, Bellerby reasoned, a good-quality globe wouldn't be difficult to find”，表明他确实预设了困难很少。这与题干陈述一致，因此答案为YES。

central_sentences.reason: surely...wouldn't be difficult 表明他预设困难少，与 YES 一致
central_sentences.analysis: Bellerby 原以为容易找到

options.text: he decided it was best to work quickly.
he became aware of an unexpected issue.
he was worried about the quality of his materials.
he nearly gave up the whole project.

questions.analysis: 题干问及Bellerby在粘贴gores时的情况，原文第五段提到他在最后阶段才发现“the paper had stretched slightly and so the final one overlapped the first”，这是一个未预料到的问题。选项B“became aware of an unexpected issue”准确描述了这一情况，因此为正确答案。

central_sentences.reason: 意外问题对应选项B。
central_sentences.analysis: 粘贴时发现纸张拉伸导致重叠，意外问题。
sentences: Right at the end of the process, he learnt that the paper had stretched slightly and so the final one overlapped the first by a centimetre (which may not seem a great deal, but when that represents 2 per cent of the Earth's diameter, it's equivalent to obliterating the Himalayas or wiping out Chile).
central_sentences.paraphrasing: became aware of unexpected issue -> learnt that the paper had stretched slightly and so the final one overlapped the first by a centimetre

central_sentences.sentence: Right at the end of the process, he learnt that the paper had stretched slightly and so the final one overlapped the first by a centimetre (which may not seem a great deal, but when that represents 2 per cent of the Earth's diameter, it's equivalent to obliterating the Himalayas or wiping out Chile).

questions.title: The reviewer mentions other globe makers of the past because

options.text: Bellerby was particularly inspired by them.
their achievements are not widely known.
Bellerby had something in common with each of them.
their difficulties could have been avoided.

questions.analysis: 题干问为何提及过去的地球仪制作者。原文第六段指出Bellerby与Martin Behaim是“kindred spirit”，并认为自己的雄心在Coronelli身上得到映照，说明他与这些历史人物有共通之处。选项C“had something in common with each of them”准确概括了这一点，因此为正确答案。

central_sentences.reason: 分别指出与 Behaim 和 Coronelli 的共通之处，支持 C
central_sentences.analysis: 提及古人因有共同点
sentences: Bellerby clearly found a kindred spirit in Martin Behaim.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: had something in common -> found a kindred spirit; unflinching ambition reflected in...Coronelli

central_sentences.sentence: Bellerby clearly found a kindred spirit in Martin Behaim.

questions.title: What point is made about Bellerby in the seventh paragraph?

options.text: He had long working relationships with numerous craftsmen.
He understands the lack of interest in traditional crafts.
He appreciates the importance of careful planning.
He regrets the loss of many globe-making skills.

questions.analysis: 第七段开头指出Bellerby的书“is also a lament for the fading away of centuries-old traditions”，并提到许多掌握技艺的工匠“have now retired or passed away”。这表明他对传统技艺的流失感到惋惜，选项D“regrets the loss of many globe-making skills”准确反映了这一观点。

central_sentences.reason: lament 和工匠离世表明他遗憾技能失传，选 D
central_sentences.analysis: 惋惜传统技艺流失
central_sentences.keywords: fading traditions, retired or passed away
sentences: Although a celebration of the revival of an ancient craft, Bellerby's book is also a lament for the fading away of centuries-old traditions.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: regrets loss of skills -> lament for the fading away...many...have now retired or passed away

central_sentences.sentence: Although a celebration of the revival of an ancient craft, Bellerby's book is also a lament for the fading away of centuries-old traditions.

questions.title: What does the reviewer say about Bellerby's book in the final paragraph?

options.text: It does not tell you how to create a profitable business.
It fails to discuss the future of globe-making.
It does not give enough details about individual customers.

questions.analysis: 最后一段提到Bellerby的书“is hardly a blueprint for commercial success”，即它并非一本商业成功指南。选项A“does not tell you how to create a profitable business”正是对此句的同义转述，因此为正确答案。

central_sentences.reason: 原文说不是商业成功指南。
central_sentences.analysis: 最后一段指出他的书并非商业成功蓝图。
central_sentences.keywords: book, final paragraph, profitable business, not blueprint
sentences: His book, beautifully illustrated with photographs of the various stages of his venture and a few illustrations of historic globes and maps, is hardly a blueprint for commercial success.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: does not tell how to create profitable business -> hardly a blueprint for commercial success

central_sentences.sentence: His book, beautifully illustrated with photographs of the various stages of his venture and a few illustrations of historic globes and maps, is hardly a blueprint for commercial success.
